title: Search Metadata, Indexing Directives, Canonical Signals, and Language Alternates · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds search discovery and indexability audit record from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of search discovery and indexability audit record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Extract title and description candidates with cardinality, location, text quality, changed-case, and stakeholder-specific non-ranking claims.
Reconcile redirects, HTTP canonical links, HTML canonical links, sitemap entries, internal links, and content identity without forcing agreement.
Parse page-level robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by applicable user agent and separate crawling from indexing and serving.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Title Description Evidence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 1940 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile search metadata, robots directives, canonical signals, language alternates, and indexability non-claims; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Extract title and description candidates with cardinality, location, text quality, changed-case, and stakeholder-specific non-ranking claims.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Indexability Decision
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-indexability-decision-gbxo73
# Competency: scraper-indexability-decision.
# Case 7739: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_indexability_decision_gbxo73(pages: int = 36, indexing_decisions: int = 36, canonical_conflicts: int = 4, alternate_sets: int = 6, unsupported_index_guarantees: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_gbxo73 = "gbxo73-37oxbg-7739"
    assert evidence_variant_gbxo73
    evidence_axes_gbxo73 = (
        "authorizationgbxo73",
        "url7739",
        "response37oxbg",
        "treegbxo737739",
        "frontier7739gbxo73",
        "report37oxbg7739",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_gbxo73)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or indexing_decisions < 0:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-count"
    if indexing_decisions != pages:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-reconciliation"
    if canonical_conflicts < 0 or alternate_sets < 1:
        return False, canonical_conflicts, "search-signal-evidence"
    if unsupported_index_guarantees > 0:
        return False, unsupported_index_guarantees, "index-guarantee"
    assert indexing_decisions == pages and unsupported_index_guarantees == 0
    return True, indexing_decisions, "evidence-based-indexability-audit"
